content.description:
Automatic backlinks when items reference each other.

## Concept

When note A links to note B via `item://`, automatically show "Linked from: Note A" on note B. Enables discovering connections you didn't explicitly create.

## Implementation Options

| Approach | Pros | Cons |
|----------|------|------|
| **Computed on render** — scan all items for references | No data changes needed, always accurate | O(N) scan on every render, slow at scale |
| **Relationship items** — links create explicit relationship items | Fast queries, explicit | Data duplication, sync issues when links change |
| **Hybrid** — markdown links computed, explicit relationships for important connections | Best of both worlds | Two systems to maintain |

## Design Questions

- Do backlinks clutter the UI? Should they be collapsed by default?
- Should backlinks show surrounding context text?
- How to handle backlink removal when a link is deleted?
- Could a `query` block handle this today? (scan `content.description` for `item://` references)

## Priority

Medium — high value for knowledge management. A `query` block could prototype this without kernel changes.
